label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me think through this step by step. The question is asking for the date one week from the last day of 1899, and then to express that date in MM/DD/YYYY format. First, I need to figure out what date the last day of 1899 is. The last day of any given year is December 31st of that year. So, the last day of 1899 would be December 31, 1899. Now, I need to add one week (7 days) to this date. Adding seven days to December 31, 1899, would take us into the next year, 1900. But here's where I need to be careful. December has 31 days. So, let me count the days carefully. If December 31, 1899 is the starting point: - Adding one day would be January 1, 1900. - Adding two days would be January 2, 1900. - Adding three days would be January 3, 1900. - Adding four days would be January 4, 1900. - Adding five days would be January 5, 1900. - Adding six days would be January 6, 1900. - Adding seven days would be January 7, 1900. So, one week from December 31, 1899 would be January 7, 1900. In MM/DD/YYYY format, that would be 01/07/1900. Now, let's look at the answer choices to see which one matches this date: (A) 02/07/1900
